stories.title: Tennessee Walks Off Texas Tech 2-1 in 9 Innings — Clarke HR Makes History (Tier 1)

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Emma Clarke was 0-for-3 entering the 9th inning. 🥎

Then she hit a walk-off solo homer to send Tennessee to the WCWS semifinals.

First walk-off win in Vols program history. Tennessee 2, Texas Tech 1.

#VolsSoftball #WCWS #NCAASoftball

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Emma Clarke now has 4 home runs in the 2026 NCAA tournament.

That's tied for 2nd most in the entire field.

She saved Tennessee's season in the 9th inning. 🥎

#VolsSoftball #WCWS #NCAASoftball

facebook.long_form:
Emma Clarke stepped to the plate in the bottom of the 9th inning with her team tied 1-1, and she was 0-for-3 on the day.

What happened next will be in Tennessee softball history books forever.

Clarke lifted a solo home run to end it — the first walk-off in WCWS history for the Vols program. Tennessee 2, Texas Tech 1. Final. The Lady Vols are in the semifinals.

Taelyn Holley had gotten Tennessee on the board first with a solo shot in the 5th inning. Texas Tech tied it before Sage Mardjetko came on in relief and held the Red Raiders at bay, setting up Clarke's legendary 9th-inning moment.

Clarke now has 4 home runs in this NCAA tournament — tied for 2nd in the entire field. Not bad for someone who had been shut down for three straight at-bats before the game was on the line.

Tennessee faces the winner of Texas vs. Nebraska in Monday's semifinal. The Vols are rolling, and Clarke's walk-off is the kind of moment that defines a championship run. 🥎
facebook.image_caption: Emma Clarke was 0-for-3. Then she hit a walk-off homer in the 9th. First walk-off in Vols WCWS history. Tennessee is in the semifinals. 🥎 #VolsSoftball #WCWS

article.headline: Emma Clarke's Walk-Off Blast Sends Tennessee to WCWS Semifinals

stories.title: Alabama Ends Nebraska's 27-Game Streak — Briski Dominates with a 1-Hitter (Tier 1)

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Jocelyn Briski threw a complete-game 1-hitter.

6 strikeouts. 0 walks. Perfect through 3.2 innings.

Alabama 5, Nebraska 1. Nebraska's 27-game winning streak is over. 🥎

#RollTide #WCWS #NCAASoftball

facebook.long_form:
All week, the question was: Can Jordy Frahm recover after throwing 133 pitches on Thursday?

Saturday night, Alabama answered it decisively.

Jocelyn Briski threw a complete-game 1-hitter — perfect through 3.2 innings, 6 strikeouts, zero walks, 83 pitches. Meanwhile, Marlie Giles hit a 3-run home run off Frahm in the very first inning to set the tone early.

Frahm lasted only 2 innings. Nebraska's coaches knew the risk. Alabama knew the opportunity. The Crimson Tide took it.

The only blemish on Briski's night: Hannah Camenzind's solo homer in the 4th — a consolation run in what ended as a 5-1 Alabama victory.

Nebraska's 27-game winning streak — the longest active streak in the country when the WCWS began — is over. But what a season the Huskers had. Getting to this stage after a 13-year WCWS drought was already extraordinary.

Alabama is 2-0 at the WCWS. They look unbeatable. Briski and Giles are playing the best softball of their lives. The Tide awaits the winner of UCLA vs. Texas Tech on Monday. 🥎
facebook.image_caption: Briski: 1 hit, 6 strikeouts, 0 walks. Giles: 4 RBI. Alabama ends Nebraska's 27-game winning streak. The Tide are rolling. 🥎 #RollTide #WCWS

Alabama Ends Nebraska's 27-Game Streak — Briski's 1-Hitter
**Classification:** NEW STORY

**Sources:**
1. Washington Post — "Marlie Giles drives in 4 runs, Jocelyn Briski allows 1 hit and Alabama beats Nebraska 5-1 at WCWS" (May 30, 2026)
2. Yahoo Sports — "Women's College World Series scores, results: Alabama dominates Nebraska, Tennessee outlasts Texas Tech" (May 30, 2026)
3. Field Level Media — "Jocelyn Briski shuts down Nebraska, leads Alabama to WCWS semifinals" (May 30, 2026)
4. Chattanooga Times Free Press — "Alabama cruises past Nebraska to remain unbeaten at WCWS" (May 30, 2026)
5. Omaha World-Herald — "'Bad News Bears for a minute': Early miscues cost Nebraska softball in WCWS loss to Alabama" (May 30, 2026)
6. Sports Illustrated/Nebraska — "Nebraska Softball Falls to Alabama After Costly First Inning" (May 30, 2026)
**Key Facts:**
- Final score: Alabama 5, Nebraska 1 [HIGH — multiple sources]
- Jocelyn Briski threw a CG 1-hitter, 6 K, 0 BB, 83 pitches (62 strikes) [HIGH — Washington Post, Field Level Media]
- Briski had a perfect game through 3.2 innings [HIGH — Field Level Media]
- Marlie Giles hit a 3-run HR off Frahm in the 1st inning (3-0 lead); added a sac fly in 3rd (4-0); total 4 RBI [HIGH — Washington Post]
- Hannah Camenzind solo HR to center field in the 4th — Nebraska's only run [HIGH — Washington Post]
- Jordy Frahm started but only pitched 2 innings (still recovering from 133-pitch outing Thursday) [HIGH — Omaha World-Herald, SI/Nebraska]
- Frahm walked the first batter, hit a batter, left a pitch up that got hit hard — Revelle noted she "didn't have her best stuff" [HIGH — Omaha World-Herald]
- Nebraska's 27-game winning streak — longest active in country — ended [HIGH — multiple sources]
- Alabama advances to Monday's semifinal 2-0 at the WCWS (56-7 overall)
**Freshness Verification:** Published May 30, 2026 — result from yesterday's game. Fresh; no prior I Love Softball coverage of this result.

stories.title: Texas vs. Nebraska — Elimination Game 9 Today at 3 PM ET on ABC (Tier 2)

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Texas vs. Nebraska. Elimination game. 3 PM on ABC. 🥎

Teagan Kavan is fresh off a 78-pitch complete game.

Jordy Frahm went 2 innings Saturday after 133 pitches Thursday.

Winner plays Tennessee on Monday. Loser goes home.

#HookEm #Huskers #NCAASoftball

facebook.long_form:
Two of the most compelling teams left in the WCWS meet today in an elimination game.

Defending champion Texas is still alive — barely. Teagan Kavan threw a 78-pitch complete-game shutout Thursday to beat Mississippi State. She's rested, sharp, and ready for today's 3 PM ABC matchup against Nebraska.

On the other side, Nebraska is battling back after Saturday's 5-1 loss to Alabama. Jordy Frahm was limited to 2 innings that game — still working through the fatigue of a 133-pitch Thursday outing. The big question: Is Frahm available today? And if so, can she be the ace Nebraska needs?

Both teams split their regular-season matchup (Texas won 15-10, lost 8-5 at UTSA Invitational in January). This is different. This is the WCWS. This is win or go home.

The winner plays Tennessee on Monday. The loser's season ends at Devon Park today.

Texas is the defending national champion. Nebraska is the feel-good story of the year. One of these seasons ends this afternoon. 🥎
facebook.image_caption: Defending champion Texas vs. Nebraska. Elimination game. 3 PM on ABC. The WCWS doesn't get bigger than this. 🥎 #HookEm #Huskers #NCAASoftball

stories.title: UCLA vs. Texas Tech — Grant and Canady Both Need a Win Tonight (Tier 2)

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Megan Grant. NiJaree Canady. Tonight. 7 PM on ESPN. 🥎

Grant has 42 home runs this season — an NCAA record.

Canady has been the best pitcher in college softball all year.

One of them doesn't play Monday. Don't miss it.

#GoBruins #WreckEm #NCAASoftball

facebook.long_form:
This is the game you do not want to miss tonight.

Megan Grant has hit 42 home runs this season. That's the NCAA single-season record she set last week, and she keeps adding to it. Grant also broke the UCLA all-time career record with her 91st home run Friday. She is having one of the greatest individual seasons in college softball history — and tonight it's all on the line.

On the other side: NiJaree Canady. The Texas Tech ace entered the 2026 WCWS as arguably the most decorated pitcher in college softball, with a 1.62 season ERA. The WCWS has been a different story — a 7.16 ERA in 14-plus innings. She allowed Tennessee's walk-off homer in her last appearance.

Canady is going to need her absolute best tonight against a UCLA lineup that can rake.

This is Grant's last shot at a national title. It's Canady's last shot too. One of them is eliminated tonight. The winner gets Alabama on Monday in the semifinals.

Appointment television. 7 PM on ESPN. 🥎
facebook.image_caption: Megan Grant (42 HRs, NCAA record) vs. NiJaree Canady (1.62 ERA all season). Two legends. Elimination game. Tonight at 7 PM on ESPN. 🥎 #GoBruins #WreckEm #WCWS

article.headline: Grant vs. Canady: UCLA and Texas Tech Meet in WCWS Must-Win Game Tonight

stories.title: WCWS Semifinal Picture — Four Teams, Monday's Matchups Set (Tier 2)

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
WCWS 2026 update:

In the semifinals: Tennessee ✅  Alabama ✅

Playing today for the final two spots:
🥎 Texas vs. Nebraska — 3 PM ABC
🥎 UCLA vs. Texas Tech — 7 PM ESPN

Championship series starts June 3.

#NCAASoftball #WCWS #CollegeSoftball

facebook.long_form:
Here's where we stand heading into Sunday's elimination games at Devon Park.

Tennessee and Alabama have punched their tickets to Monday's semifinals. Tennessee won on Emma Clarke's walk-off in the 9th inning (Game 7). Alabama dominated Nebraska 5-1 behind Briski's 1-hitter and Giles' 4 RBI night (Game 8).

Today, two more teams join them — or go home trying.

🥎 Game 9: Texas vs. Nebraska — 3 PM ET on ABC
Winner faces Tennessee on Monday

🥎 Game 10: UCLA vs. Texas Tech — 7 PM ET on ESPN
Winner faces Alabama on Monday

The Monday semifinal matchups will be set by tonight. Then the championship series begins Wednesday, June 3.

Four teams will still be playing on Monday morning. Six are playing today. And two go home before the day ends.

That's the WCWS. 🥎
facebook.image_caption: Tennessee ✅ Alabama ✅ Two more spots left. Texas, Nebraska, UCLA, and Texas Tech play today to fill them. WCWS semifinals are Monday. Championship series starts June 3. 🥎 #NCAASoftball #WCWS

article.headline: WCWS 2026: Tennessee and Alabama Are In. Four Teams Fight for Two More Spots Today.
